entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - External logging to Datadog Using OpenTelemetry
entry.content:
Hi,

how is this open telemetry?

We connected to datadog, but we are not seeing any Trace IDs or Spans.
This is no tracing, its only pushing the logs to Datadog.

entry.title: Unable to access BI reports via LPD_CUST configuration after upgrade to S/4HANA 2023.
entry.content: Hi Team, We are facing an issue after upgrading our system from S/4HANA 2021 to S/4HANA 2023. Before Upgrade (Working Scenario): - We were accessing BI (external system) reports from Fiori Launchpad. - Configuration was done using: • Application Type: SAP Fiori App using LPD_CUST • Transaction: LPD_CUST - A Launchpad Role (e.g., ZSAP_BI) and Instance (ANALYTICS) was created. - URLs for BI reports (BOE OpenDocument links) were maintained in LPD_CUST. - Corresponding Target Mapping was created in Fiori Launchpad Designer. - When users clicked the tile: → System redirected to BI system → User entered credentials → Report opened successfully After Upgrade (S/4HANA 2023): - The same configuration is either not working or not supported. - We understand that Launchpad Designer and LPD_CUST based integrations are deprecated/obsolete. - We are unable to identify the correct approach/tool to reconfigure this setup. Current Requirement: We need to enable Fiori tiles to launch external BI reports (BOE URLs) similar to the previous setup.

entry.title: Re: Is there any way to get published v4 apis through api?
entry.content:
I found it by myself.

Communication scenario: SAP_COM_0181 (OData Metadata Integration)

Service URL: 

https://myXXXXXX-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/iwfnd/config/default/iwfnd/catalog/0002/ServiceGroups?$expand=DefaultSystem($expand=Services)

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Vibe coding SAP HANA Cloud Machine Learning with BAS, Python, Claude and Clin
entry.content:
It's quite amazing how many different ways the LLMs are finding to create the predict DataFrame that holds the future 6 months. Here are a few approaches that I saw. Feel free to post some more, if you come across any further

Pandas creating the dates, which are then uploaded to HANA Cloud

 

A union of Dummy SELECTs

 

My favorite so far, using SERIES_GENERATE_INTEGER, which I hadn't known about...

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
SII Spain – Conflict between Operation Date/Fiscal Year Period and invoice date in SD sales orders:
entry.content:
Dear community;

We are working with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the SII (Suministro Inmediato de Información) integration for the Spanish Tax Agency (AEAT). We have encountered a recurring issue affecting several types of SD sales orders, and we would like to know whether a standard solution exists or if this is a known gap.

System behavior:
For customer invoices (F2) generated from SD sales orders (services), the system determines the Operation Date and Fiscal Year/Period fields sent to the SII by reading the "Requested Service Date" from the sales order. If this field is empty, the system falls back to the invoice date on the sales order. The Invoice Issue Date, on the other hand, always takes the actual invoice issue date.

The problem:
We have three operational scenarios where the requested service date on the sales order differs from the invoice issue date, causing the Operation Date and Fiscal Year/Period to diverge from the invoice date and triggering conflicts when submitting to the SII:

  1. Services billed in advance: rental contracts where invoicing occurs before the service period begins. The service date falls in the month after the invoice is issued.
  2. Rebilling of prior periods: penalties or demurrage charges billed in the current month but relating to services rendered in previous months.
  3. Material sales orders with an adjusted value date: the value date is modified to control payment due dates correctly, and the system uses it to determine the Fiscal Year/Period sent to the SII.

In all three cases, the current workaround is manually editing the Tax Fulfillment Date on the posted invoice, which the accounting team needs to eliminate.

What we have already checked:
We have not found any SSCUI or standard configuration option in Public Cloud Edition that allows controlling the source of the Operation Date / Fiscal Year/Period fields on SD invoices, beyond leaving the requested service date blank on the sales order — which is not viable for all scenarios.

The question:
Has anyone resolved any of these scenarios in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition without relying on manual post-posting corrections? Is there standard configuration we may have missed, or is this a gap?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: VIES Validation - Business Partner
entry.content:
Hi Marcel,

Yes, there is a standard option for this. VIES validation can be triggered when Business Partner master data is saved, provided that the relevant validation spot is activated.

For VIES validation, the supported trigger points are:

  1. Business Partner master data save
  2. Manual/periodic validation using the app Online Validation for Multiple Partners
  3. Sales order creation
  4. Billing document creation

So, for your requirement to validate the VAT number when a new Business Partner is created, a custom extension is not required just to trigger the VIES validation. The relevant Business Partner validation spot needs to be activated/configured.

Best,

Anne

Stefan

Dear Elena,

thank you, I have tried he suggested solution (I hope I got it right), and only created the WM inspection usage decision for a partial quantity, then Saved the inspection (after the input on the main screen as well regrading usage dec. code, since otherwise it is not letting me to press Save as I mentioned):

 

(The full quantity of the inspection lot is 10 pcs.)

Although the results are the same regarding the follow-up action quantity:

- The system blocked the whole amount

When I went back to make a 2nd partial decision on the remaining stock, the system is not letting me do so due to "usage decision has been made already".

Am I doing something wrong, or missing something?

Thank you very much!

BR,

Adam

Thank you.

entry.title: Join Our Live Session: Quality Planning in SAP S/4HANA Quality Management
entry.content:
Date: September 29, 2026
Time: 10:00 AM – 11:30 AM

Quality Planning is a fundamental component of SAP S/4HANA Quality Management, providing the framework that determines what is inspected, how inspections are carried out, and when they are triggered within business processes.

In this live session, we will explore the key elements of Quality Planning and their role in controlling and standardizing quality inspection processes. Participants will gain an understanding of how inspection types, master inspection characteristics, and inspection plans work together to ensure consistent quality management across procurement and production processes.

The session will also demonstrate how these planning elements drive the automatic creation of inspection lots during logistical events such as goods receipts and production confirmations, enabling efficient and controlled quality execution.

Whether you are new to SAP Quality Management or looking to strengthen your understanding of quality planning concepts, this session will provide valuable insights into the master data structures that support effective quality processes in SAP S/4HANA.

What You Will Learn

By the end of this session, you will be able to:

  • Explain the role of Quality Planning within SAP S/4HANA Quality Management.
  • Describe how inspection types in the material master determine when quality inspections are triggered.
  • Identify the purpose of master inspection characteristics in defining inspection requirements.
  • Explain how inspection plans structure and standardize quality inspection processes.
  • Understand how quality planning elements enable the automatic creation of inspection lots during logistical events.

entry.title: Business Partner Migration from R3 into S4 Hana public cloud
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are designing the customer and supplier master concept for an S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition migration and would value experience from anyone who has solved this.

Scenario: we need to harmonize legacy R/3 customers (debtors) and suppliers (creditors) into a single Business Partner, but keep the original customer and supplier numbers as the operative KUNNR and LIFNR, because several 3rd-party systems use these numbers as primary keys. The legacy debtor and creditor ranges overlap (debtor 123 and creditor 123 can be different entities), so same-number for the legacy population is not possible.

Our preferred target, but we have never implemented it:
- The BP gets its own neutral internal number.
- KUNNR and LIFNR keep their different legacy numbers via external assignment (Flexible Grouping, "Same Numbers" off in SSCUI 102741 and 102742).
- A dual-role entity is one BP with, for example, BP 50001, KUNNR 123, LIFNR 456 (three different numbers).
- New business partners going forward use same-number (BP = KUNNR = LIFNR).

What we tried so far: we deactivated "Same Numbers" and used the two-step approach (migrate Customer, then "Supplier - extend existing record by new org levels"). In our tests the BP, customer and supplier always end up with the identical number, so we keep getting same-number behavior and cannot produce three different numbers on one BP. References we worked from: SAP Note 2748819 and KBA 3393756.

We also evaluated the alternatives, but each has a significant downside, which is why we prefer the approach above:
- Storing the legacy numbers as BP identification numbers/types (the SAP-standard route): would force custom resolution logic in every single 3rd-party interface, an anti-pattern for our landscape.
- Separate Business Partners for the debtor and the creditor role: goes against the one-BP-per-entity model, and we hit known issues with duplicate identification numbers in another project.
- Customer-side data cleansing and renumbering: would mean changing the primary keys in the downstream systems, which is exactly what we must avoid.

Questions:
1) Is our preferred target (one BP, different KUNNR and LIFNR, neutral BP number) actually achievable in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and would you recommend it?
2) What is the correct configuration and migration sequence to end up with three different numbers on one BP? What are we most likely missing, given our attempts keep collapsing to same-number?
3) Any pitfalls at scale (100k+ records), e.g. CVIV_UI 6 "Enter External Number" or CVI_EI011/CVI_EI041?
4) For a dual-role entity, is Customer first plus "Supplier - extend existing record by new org levels" the right path, and how do you keep the two numbers distinct without a need for special logic in the underlying interfaces?

Thanks a lot for any working configuration, steps, or lessons learned.

Best,
Isabella

entry.title: Re: Mass update Settlement Dates in Condition Contract
entry.content:
@priya_sshekhar  

You can propose a solution in either of the following two ways:

1)Ask users to update the settlement calendars monthly for both the Delta Accruals and Final Settlement sections in the Condition Contract manually when creating the contract. By doing so, the settlement calendars will be generated automatically each time the contract is saved.
2)Adjust the configuration under Node: Specify Settlement Settings for Condition Contract Types. Select the relevant contract type and maintain the settlement calendars in the configuration. This will enable the settlement calendars to be generated automatically for both Delta Accruals and Final Settlement. (This way you can Minimize the manual maintenance of adding calendars to contract manually)

entry.title: Price Group Condition ZA00 not working
entry.content: P80-658: group Pricing not working ZA00 condition

entry.title: Drop shipment in report configuration of SAP Responsible Design and Production
entry.content:
Dear community,

I have questions regarding the link between material documents and invoices and on how to configure reports in SAP Responsible Design and Production in the context of drop shipment. 

In the material document object I have the fields Reference Document, Sales Order, Purchase Order, and Production Order. Does that mean that the invoice is referred to via the Reference Document? So all the companies involved in the physical goods movement and in the invoice process need to be replicated to SAP RDP? Is the supplier tax number taken from the matdoc supplier (shipped-from party) or from the invoiced supplier?

When creating a report configuration, the company code and delivery document type is mandatory. What is the company code used for exactly? Is it used to filter for products with company code > sales organization > products? Is it used to filter for material/delivery documents? Do I indicate company codes that are involved in shipping (company code in matdocs/deliveries) or selling (company code in invoice)?

Thank you and best regards,

Jessy

entry.title: Re: the impact when switch a RFC from non-unicode to unicode
entry.content:
In this case, as both sides (LUP and GRC) are Unicode systems, there will probably be less problems when switching the connection to Unicode than there were with Non-Unicode:

a) Unicode supports more characters than any of the Non-Unicode languages, so there will be less invalid characters and therefore less #-signs in the received data.

b) The performance will be significantly better, because we are saving two codepage conversions! Consider what happens during RFC communication, when two Unicode systems communicate: both systems already have all data in Unicode format.

When making an RFC call with a Non-Unicode destination:

  • sending system takes the data in Unicode format (e.g. from a DB table etc.)
  • then converts it to the NUC codepage
  • then sends it over the network connection
  • the receiving system then converts it from NUC codepage back to Unicode
  • then processes it/stores it in DB tables etc.

When the destination is switched to Unicode, this process looks like this:

  • sending system takes the data from DB table
  • sends it over the network connection as is
  • the receiving system receives it already in Unicode format
  • and then processes/stores it in DB tables as is.

Neither the sending nor the receiving system needs to perform a codepage conversion, and codepage conversions are expensive operations!

When 'ON' they don't.

entry.title: 財務諸表バージョンや利益センタグループの有効期間での表示内容確認
entry.content:
本件は以前ケース登録したSR:637765/2026 の回答内容に関する詳細確認となります。
以下に詳細確認の内容を記載します。
SR:637765/2026 の回答内容に以下の記載がございました。
ー試算表アプリでは、財務諸表バージョンはレポート階層としてサポートされていません。
ーアプリは総勘定元帳の勘定階層のみを使用します。

上記部分でサポートされないとは何を意味するのでしょうか。
※残高試算表で財務諸表バージョンは階層として指定は可能になっています。

例として、現在、財務諸表バージョン：AAAA　有効期間：①2025/1/1~2025/12/31、②2026/1/1~9999/12/31で①②ともにステータス：有効としているため、このステータス：有効を財務諸表バージョン内で1つにすれば有効なバージョンで出力できるのではと思っています。

entry.title: Re: SAP .NET Connector not working anymore
entry.content:
Why do you think, this is a SAP .NET Connector problem?

  • The error message comes from the operating system, not from NCo.
  • And apparently the error happens inside an ODBC driver, not inside NCo!

Of course, as Markus already pointed out, the exhaustive memory consumption might happen anywhere, even in NCo, but there is no proof yet! Basically, there are multiple possibilities:

  • the exhaustive memory consumption might happen inside another process, not even in the Power BI process. The Power BI process just runs into this error, because that other process has already consumed all the memory that the operating system has to offer...
  • The out-of-memory situation happens, because the Power BI is trying to process a data set that is too big for the machine. In that case, it can be fixed by installing more RAM on the machine, or by dividing the one big data set into several smaller ones.
  • The Power BI program has a memory leak. But such a leak can be anywhere, in the ODBC driver used here, in NCo, in Power BI coding, etc.

In short: this problem first needs to be investigated by a Windows expert on OS level.

entry.title: Re: SBPA Rules Runtime API returns 403 "bpm.rulesruntime....
entry.content:
Hi,

On the path, make sure you have the correct Shared Environment ID. The URL should be "/public/rule/runtime/rest/v2/rule-services?environmentId=<SBPAEnvironmentID>" and that the rule is deployed to the specified Environment.

Additionally, create an API trigger for the Decision. You can find the details in the documentation

Regarding the header content, it should be "api-key" and not "Irpa-api-key". As one should not have secrets in the code, see also the documentation on setting up things to use the API key from a CAP application.

Hope it helps.

entry.title: Transport Request tab/Page is not showing
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are using solution manager charms for the Transport Request creation and movement to test,PRD.
how ever now we are not finding the Transport Request page/Tab in the charms for config item YYMF only.
Please provide solution how can we get it.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Transport Request tab/Page is not showing
entry.content:
Hi,

Regarding your issue, you need to go to your YYMF transaction change document->click button 'configure page' on top right, if you don't see this button, click 'Personalize' at the top of the page->in it you could enable the configuration mode by check the checkbox. When you click 'configure page', in section 'View'->you will see available assignment block 'Transport management'->move it to display column->save&close the popup window. Refresh your YYMF page, you will see the Transport management AB, there you could create TRs then.

BR

entry.title: Re: SAP .NET Connector not working anymore
entry.content: I don't think, a SAP ticket should be opened for this. As I wrote in my comment above, the problem could be anywhere, in another process running simultaneously to Power BI, in the ODBC driver, in Power BI itself or in NCo. First a Windows expert should try to narrow this down, and only if the evidence really points to a memory leak in NCo, a SAP ticket should be opened.

entry.title: SA ID and Custom fields (ERF, Portion, GIS Key) searchable in Agent Desktop search bar
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working on an SAP Service Cloud v2 implementation for a municipal customer and need agents to be able to search by the following identifiers directly from the Agent Desktop search bar:

  • South African ID number (13-digit national identifier) - primary requirement
  • ERF number (cadastral land parcel ID)
  • Portion number
  • GIS Key (internal reference linking a property to our GIS/location data)

SAP Support confirmed there's no standard field under Individual Customer for the ID number when checked via Adaptation mode, and noted that even a custom field added there wouldn't necessarily be visible , let alone searchable , on Agent Desktop.

 

 I have added a request on SAP Influence under this heading: SA ID and Custom fields (ERF, Portion, GIS Key) searchable in Agent Desktop search bar.

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide to Upport and Downport in SAP Using TCode UDO
entry.content: How do you get UDO? Tcode UDO doesn't exist by default. What note is intended to be installed to get UDO?

entry.title: Re: UAE eInvoicing - Company Code set up for Group Tax entities - VAT TRN & Additional Parameter
entry.content:
Does anyone face this common legal set up for intra-group companies in UAE?

Can anyone confirm our understanding:

1. The T001-STCEG should be filled in with the VAT Corporate Tax TRN of each company code.

2. SAP does not have a specific parameter in additional data of company code maintenance in OBY6 which corresponds to VAT Group Tax TRN (which is common for both our company codes).

3. VAT Group Tax TRN does not play any role in SAP UAE e-Invoicing Solution.

entry.title: Datasphere P&L hierarchy
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a Levels Hierarchy in SAP Datasphere that is consumed live in SAP Analytics Cloud.

The aggregation logic is derived from PaPM, while the total nodes (such as EBIT, Total Revenue, and Total Cost) are created in Datasphere within my model.

Currently, these nodes are displayed at the top level of the hierarchy. I would like to reorganize the hierarchy so that they appear further down in the structure, under a different parent node, while preserving their aggregation behavior.

In addition, I may need to introduce nodes such as Total Revenue External and Total Revenue Group beneath Total Revenue.

Is this possible within a Datasphere Levels Hierarchy, or would it require a different hierarchy design/modeling approach?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Supply Protection and Segmentation
entry.content:
Hello,
for mass-uploads the API "Supply Availability Protection Plan" (Technical name: API_SUPAVAILYPROTPLAN_G4BA) is the best option. There is no step-by-step description but an example for a Batch-Request. 

Best regards,

Arno

entry.title: Re: WE zu Lohnbearbeitung mit MIGO funktioniert nicht

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors and Contingent Workers
entry.content:
Hi @shrutithakkar and all others,

Thank you for your great work!

We differentiate in the Worker Type between "Standard Contingent Worker", "Consultants" and some other types. 

But the Label only shows "Contingent Worker". Is it possible to change the Label depending on the Worker Type or will all Worker Types allways be labled as "CWs"? 

Does anyone else have different Worker Types of CWs? 

Thank you for your support 🙂 

BR, Bernadette 

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Employee Profile

entry.title: Re: SQL Console executes and fails to execute identical query
entry.content:
Hi @VXLozano ,

Thanks for taking an interest. The think is not the query itself but the bug which parses the SQL from SQL Console. ADT team created a bug two or three updates ago, and they are not fixing it. 

Here is the same query just differently formatted. One is fine and another is erroneous:

Fine ✅

SELECT vbfa~vbelv,vbfa~posnv
FROM vbfa
JOIN likp ON likp~vbeln = vbfa~vbeln
WHERE likp~erdat >= '20260101'
  AND likp~wadat_ist = '00000000'
  AND vbfa~vbtyp_n = 'J'
GROUP BY vbfa~vbelv, vbfa~posnv
HAVING MIN( vbfa~vbeln ) <> MAX( vbfa~vbeln )

Erroneous ❌

SELECT 
  vbfa~vbelv
  ,vbfa~posnv
FROM vbfa
JOIN likp ON likp~vbeln = vbfa~vbeln
WHERE likp~erdat >= '20260101'
  AND likp~wadat_ist = '00000000'
  AND vbfa~vbtyp_n = 'J'
GROUP BY vbfa~vbelv, vbfa~posnv
HAVING MIN( vbfa~vbeln ) <> MAX( vbfa~vbeln )

Error that is reported to not have any sense:

 

Difference

Only difference is format. I placed columns in separate lines in SELECT clause.

So, parser is failing here. 🙃 I'm just surprised that ADT team haven't fixed it for so long as the tool is pretty good, regarding SAP environment, and it's probably widely used.

Regards,
Nemanja

entry.title: Re: The Future of ABAP is Here: VS Code ADT, Zero-Config MCP, and AI Co-pilots
entry.content:
I browsed a dozen blogs like this, where VSC is being sold for ABAP.

I don't see a single reason to put a backend language in a frontend tool.

If it is about putting everything in the same editor, then by that logic, instructions for plugging in network cables should be in VSC too.

Imo using VSC for ABAP, violates a core software engineering principle: everything (VSC, Eclipse, Putty for Linux servers) should be responsible for itself; reaching high encapsulation.

entry.title: System Based Certification for SAP Integrated Business Planning
entry.content:
Starting in 2026, SAP is introducing brand‑new system‑based certifications.
These updated exams consist of real, hands‑on tasks executed directly in an SAP system. The result: a much stronger focus on applied skills and real‑world capability. In the area of Asset Management, we developed a certification for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

How to Prepare Successfully

Explore the official Learning Journey (free on learning.sap.com)

Implementing SAP Integrated Business Planning

Get hands‑on practice

Use the available hands‑on training systems to run through exercises, demos, and end‑to‑end scenarios.
This practical experience is key for the new system‑based exam format. Hands-on systems can be found in the Learning Journey.

Join the “Get Certified!” Live Sessions

SAP experts provide guidance, highlight important focus areas, and answer your questions — perfect for a confident exam day. Join the sessions or watch the recordings:

Get Certified: SAP Certified - SAP Integrated Business Planning 

Why a system‑based certification?

Because real skills matter.
This new exam format demonstrates not only what you know, but what you can do inside SAP IBP.
A strong signal for customers, partners, and your own professional development.

Ready to boost your skills?

With Learning Journeys, hands‑on systems, and expert Live Sessions, you have everything you need to succeed in the new SAP IBP certification in 2026.

entry.title: Authorization of Supplier Invoice Operation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I got a customer request of authorization setting about supplier invoice operation:

Purchaser from purchase department can create supplier invoice, but can not post the invoice. Only users from finance department can post the supplier invoice.

I created a business role Z_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT (from SAP_BR_AP_ACCOUNTANT). If I assign this role to purchaser, the purchaser will be able to create and post supplier invoice at same time, which is not acceptable.

So how to create business roles or set restrictions to meet this request, thanks.

Best Regards,

Chris

entry.title: Re: Blog 3 - From BW 7.5 to SAP BDC: Reusing Extractors, Custom Logic, and Models Standard Extracto
entry.content:
Great Post but for me there is a big "why?"

For me just the question: I switch now from 7.5 a 20 Years old technology, using ODP Framework to load data over divers extractors like:

0CO_OM_WBS_1_D
0CO_OM_WBS_6
0CO_OM_NWA_2
0CO_OM_NAE_2
0CO_OM_OPA_1
0CO_PC_ACT_03

This is ECC R/3 Technology and now I have BDC and Replication Flows and CDS View as the new standard. Why not moving from Old ODP/ECC R3 Technology straight forward to ACDOCA especially for Finance and also especially if your source system is already on S4?

Yes it is the "easier" way and maybee you are faster but you migrate old Architecture thinking directly into a complete new system? This I don't get atm.

entry.title: New course - Discovering AI in SAP Integrated Business Planning
entry.content:
What Is This Course About?

Discovering AI in SAP Integrated Business Planning

Explore how SAP’s Business AI strategy enhances SAP IBP through innovative interaction patterns and transactional capabilities, primarily driven by the Joule copilot.
You’ll learn how embedded classical and generative AI refine demand forecasting and inventory optimization processes.
Discover how Joule and AI tools effectively manage supply chain disruptions while clarifying supply optimization results.
This course also discusses the product vision, showcasing the future of intelligent agents in planning automation.

What You'll Learn

Unit 1: Exploring SAP Business AI Understand the paradigm shift from traditional ERP to AI-infused enterprise software and discover how SAP is delivering relevant, reliable, and responsible AI capabilities.

Unit 2: Exploring Key Components and Applications of SAP Business AI Dive into the four pillars of SAP Business AI: Robotic Process Automation, Machine Learning, Generative AI, and Situation Handling integrated through SAP Business Technology Platform.

Unit 3: Introducing the Generative AI Copilot Joule Meet Joule—SAP's intelligent AI companion that transforms daily work through transactional, navigational, informational, and analytical interactions across enterprise applications.

Unit 4: Introducing Agentic AI and Joule Agents Explore the next evolution of automation where intelligent agents autonomously handle complex workflows, learn from outcomes, and collaborate to solve business challenges.

Unit 5: Introducing Business AI in SAP IBP Discover how AI transforms integrated business planning with intelligent data handling, automated insights, and connected planning processes.

Unit 6: Leveraging Joule for Enhanced Planning Learn practical techniques for using Joule to streamline planning workflows, generate insights, and make data-driven decisions within SAP IBP.

Unit 7: Applying AI in Demand and Inventory Planning Master AI-driven demand forecasting with automatic preprocessing, 20+ algorithms, forecast explainability, dynamic lead time prediction, service level optimization, and safety stock intelligence.

Unit 8: Enhancing Supply Planning and Exception Management with AI Analyze optimization results with Joule's explainability features, respond to supplier disruptions, and leverage AI-guided scenario analysis for faster decision-making.

Unit 9: The Future of AI in SAP IBP Intelligent Agents Discover the roadmap for autonomous AI agents that will revolutionize planning through autonomous decision-making, automated configuration, rapid disruption response, and root-cause analysis.

Getting Started

Ready to transform your supply chain planning? This course is your gateway to becoming an AI-fluent supply chain professional.

entry.title: Direct Method Cash Flow Statement with Basic Cash Management Scope?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

Is it possible to generate a Cash Flow Statement using the Direct Method with the Basic Cash Management scope (BFA/BFB) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, with values being populated?

Or is the Full Cash Management scope required to generate the Direct Method Cash Flow Statement with values?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Client Authentication EKU deprecation
entry.content:
Hi @DavidDomokos , thanks for sharing this valuable blog.

Would like to confirm the steps below is the same what you have demonstrated in the blog to generate the signed key via destination service API based on the created private key via the Open SSL.

 

And also to retrieve the PEM file, we could also achieve from the UI, and just merge the child, intermediates and root cert in the notepad before generating the pfx file via OpenSsl.

Any concern if we generate the PFX cert directly in BTP and also leveraging BTP to generate the private key instead?

entry.title: Re: CPI HTTP Receiver always returns 400 “Syntax Error” a...
entry.content: We solved it using a Groovy script in CPI and handled the web service call programmatically instead of using the approach discussed here.

entry.title: Extend MD_BUSINESSPARTNER_SRV with a additional ValueHelp
entry.content:
Hello dear forum,

We are currently in the middle of migrating from ECC to S/4 (brownfield approach). We want to use the app Manage Business Partner Master Data and have encountered an issue. The field AcctsReceivablePledgingCode (Table: TCESSION – Field: CESSION_KZ) is not visible in the UI by default, but as described in Note 3462395, it can be made visible via UI adaptation – which we have already done. Unfortunately, it appears as a free-text field without an associated value help.

Therefore, we attempted to create an interface and a corresponding consumption view for the value help and to added this via Metadata Extension to I_BusinessPartnerCustCoTP. 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZIPLEDINGIND'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface view Pleding Indicator'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true


@AbapCatalog.preserveKey:true
@VDM.viewType: #BASIC

@ObjectModel: { dataCategory: #VALUE_HELP,
                representativeKey: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode',
                usageType.sizeCategory: #S,
                usageType.dataClass: #ORGANIZATIONAL,
                usageType.serviceQuality: #A,
                supportedCapabilities: [#VALUE_HELP_PROVIDER, #SEARCHABLE_ENTITY],
                modelingPattern: #VALUE_HELP_PROVIDER }


@ClientHandling.algorithm: #SESSION_VARIABLE

@Search.searchable: true
@Consumption.ranked: true



define view ZI_PledingIndicator
  as select from tcession
    inner join   tcessiont on  tcession.cession_kz = tcessiont.cession_kz
                           and tcession.company    = tcessiont.company
{
  tcession.company        as CompanyCode,
  @search.defaultSearchElement: true
  @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
  @search.ranking: #HIGH
  tcession.cession_kz as AcctsReceivablePledgingCode,
  @Semantics.text: true
  @search: { defaultSearchElement: true, ranking: #LOW }
  @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
  tcessiont.cession_text  as Text
}
where
  tcessiont.langu = $session.system_language;
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZCPLEDINGIND'
@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@VDM.viewType: #CONSUMPTION
@Search.searchable: true

@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality: #C
@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XL
@ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass: #MIXED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption View Pleding Indicator'


define view ZC_PLEDINGINDICATOR as select from ZI_PledingIndicator
association [0..1] to I_CompanyCode as _CompanyCode on  $projection.CompanyCode = _CompanyCode.CompanyCode
{
     @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
       @search.ranking: #HIGH
       @ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_CompanyCode'
   key CompanyCode,
    @search.defaultSearchElement: true
       @search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
       @search.ranking: #HIGH
       @ObjectModel.text.element: ['Text']
   key AcctsReceivablePledgingCode,
    Text,
    _CompanyCode
}
@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
annotate entity C_BusinessPartnerCustCo
    with 
{
   @EndUserText.label: 'AR Pledging Indicator (Test)'
   @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity: { name: 'ZC_PLEDINGINDICATOR', element: 'AcctsReceivablePledgingCode'},
                                  additionalBinding: [{element: 'CompanyCode', localElement: 'CompanyCodeForEdit', usage: #FILTER}]}]
  AcctsReceivablePledgingCode;
    
}

 

However, the value help does not appear in the app, but changing End-User Labels is working fine for example.

Is the value help not showing up, because it is not part of the underlying virtual data model?

If yes, do you have any ideas on how we can implement this?

Unfortunately, this field is linked to many processes and additional custom codings on our side.

Thank you in advance for your support!

Regards 

Kai

entry.title: Add Custom Fields in Look Up Retail Products app
entry.content:
I want to add custom fields under Information tab of Look Up Retail Products app.

 

I create custom fields in Custom Fields app with Business Context: Look up product information in retail stores (RFM_STORE_LKPPROD).

But from AdaptUI, custom field is not appear. Why?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Creating and Activating an RFC Connection for SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Prerequisites

  • You have the drccApiUrl endpoint from your service binding. The drccApiUrl is a concatenation of host name and path, in the form https://<host name>/<path>. (See the Getting the URL for a Service Binding section for how to retrieve it.)
  • You have already configured the OAuth 2.0 client in your business system and noted the configuration (profile) name.

Part 1 — Creating the RFC Connection (SM59)

You create a standard or country/region-specific Remote Function Call (RFC) connection.

  1. In your business system, open the RFC Destinations (SM59) transaction.
  2. Choose Create.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  

     

  3. In the Create Destination pop-up, enter a name in the Destination field.
  4. For Connection Type, select G — HTTP Connection to External Server and confirm.

     

  5. On the Technical Settings tab, fill in the fields as follows:

     

  6. Go to the Logon & Security tab.
  7. (Relevant only if you authenticated using OAuth 2.0 and you have SAP S/4HANA 2021 or higher, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.) In the Logon Procedure section, choose OAuth Settings. In the pop-up, for the Profile field, use F4 help to select the configuration name you created when configuring the OAuth 2.0 client.

     

  8. In the Security Options section, set SSL to Active, then choose the PSE according to your authentication method:

    • Client Certificate: choose the SSL Client PSE you created to establish trust between the cloud edition and your business system.

    • OAuth 2.0: choose the SSL Client (Anonymous) PSE.

       

       

  9. Save your entries.
  10. (Optional) To test the connection, choose the Connection Test tab. The expected status depends on your setup:

     

Part 2 — Activating the Standard RFC Connection (GCO_RFC_CONF)

Once the destination exists, you activate it.

  1. In your business system, open the RFC Activation (GCO_RFC_CONF) transaction.
  2. Choose New Entries.                                                                                                                                                                                                              

     

  3. In the RFC Destination field, use the value help to select the destination you created in Part 1.

     

  4.  Confirm the destination and save your entries.

     

  5. Use the Display → Change button to switch to edit mode.
  6. Select the checkbox in the RFC Active column to activate the destination.
  7. Save your entries.

entry.title: Re: T.Code for Print preview of PR
entry.content: You need to customize that, at our company we're using YMF01 for saving PR as a PDF rather then printing.

entry.title: Re: SAP field history is active however a previously typed entries cannot be selected.
entry.content: Same issue here. Users say it worked before. Latest change was Update of Unified Rendering Patch to version 2605.

entry.title: Output Determination Issue (Dispatch Time)
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have identified a strange behavior during the message output process , after changing the Purchase Order, despite the correct settings being maintained in transaction NACE.

In the details, for the order type ZTRA – Stock Transfer Order, for which changes have been applied in transaction NACE, the system continues to store the field “Dispatch Time” with value “3 - Send with application own transaction” instead of “4 - Send immediately (when saving the application)”, when a change is applied to the Purchase Order. As a consequence, the email is not automatically sent. Please find attached the evidences:

Comparison with Other Order Types: For order types:

  • ZODA – Standard PO
  • ZGEN – No-stock & Services

the system behaves as expected: upon modification of the Purchase Order, the output is determined with Dispatch Time = 4, and the email is successfully sent:

All Purchase Orders ZTRA, ZODA, and ZGEN use the same output type: “ZNIE – Purchase Order via email”:

The NACE settings for order type ZTRA (order type with issue) are as follows:

The NACE settings for order type ZODA (working order type) are as follows:

Could you please clarify why the output message for ZTRA is always set to Dispatch Time = 3, even though in NACE it is configured as 4?

Thank you in advance for your support,

Maria

entry.title: Exception Subprocess for IDoc Receiver Adapter
entry.content:
Hi,

We are developing a scenario, where we have an ECC system receiving IDoc from Cloud Integration through Cloud Connector. We use a request-reply shape with IDoc receiver adapter. We have an exception subprocess wrapped around the IDoc receiver adapter. However, we observe that when the call has failed (IDoc data issue, or Cloud connector is down, for example), the flow does not get into the exception subprocess; rather it simply fails at the IDoc adapter step. Screenshot of our configuration is given below. We have tried to wrap the IDoc receiver into a separate local integration process, as per suggestion from this link, to no avail. We tried to go through the help documentation, but there is no mention of this behavior as well. Any hint on this behavior will be appreciated.

Thanks,

Sid

entry.title: Re: Supply Protection and Segmentation
entry.content: so the only way to do it is to write UI5 coding correct?

entry.title: My Leave Request On behalf of
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are using BADI "HCMFAB_B_COMMON" to manage customized method "GET_DIRECT_REPORTSD"  and "EMPLOYEE_IS_MANAGER" in FIORI TILE "MyLeaveRequest"

Looks good to get "On behalf of" functionnality activated in " MyLeaveRequest" service but after selecting on behalf employee in the list, we faced error gateway message "No authorization to display data of employee number" in SAP Gateway Application Log

Altern ID : /SAP/HCMFAB_LEAVE_REQUEST_CR_SRV_0001

Operation : read

Object : /IWFND/

Error code : HCMFAB_COMMON001

Checking authorisation trace did not show any differencies between standard test and customized test.

What is the core test raising this gateway message ? does it related to permissions in PFCG ?

Thanks for your support

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 17 - Schema evolution testing
entry.content: feab6352cbdbe371cd30efebe70bc045bb739be2b01c8c172415617a740a2571

entry.title: Bug in ABAP Language
entry.content:
I just discovered my first bug in the ABAP Programming language introduced with SAP NetWeaver 7.4.

Some of our customers are still on very old releases and the bug just came up when they upgraded their system.

Prior to SAP NetWeaver 7.4, you were able to do the following:

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <result> type USR02.
DATA: results type table of USR02.

append initial line to results assigning <result>.

select * from usr02 into corresponding fields of <result>.
  append initial line to results assigning <result>.
endselect.

This code does exactly what you would expect it to do on a release lower than NW 7.4 - It appends all entries of USR02 to the table RESULTS.

However, starting with NW 7.4 the following happens:

  1. An initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the first row of RESULTS.
  2. <RESULT> is populated with the first row of USR02, therefore the first row of RESULTS now also contains the data of USR02.
  3. A new initial line is appended to RESULTS and <RESULT> is assigned. <RESULT> now points to the second row of RESULTS.
  4. A new row of USR02 is selected. <RESULT> does not get populated, however the first row of RESULTS gets changed. The second row of RESULTS remains empty.

...

This is clearly a bug.

My guess:

Prior to NW 7.4, a pointer was passed to the select, now, the field symbol is no longer handled as a pointer within the select, instead the address of the work area is passed to the select.

This is confusing behaviour, as the same field symbols used in INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF and between SELECT and ENDSELECT now point to different addresses. This should not happen.

I suggest, that either the compiler will error when having to compile this, or that the previous, correct behaviour is restored.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Proxy generation support with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Great Post, this was such a big help when configuring things!
A Problem we had: The OAuth2 token was too big for our S4 system to handle, so we had to restrict the scope to shorten the token. Only two scopes are actually needed for ESR Proxy:

it!x12345.Default
it!x12345.WebToolingWorkspace.Read

(change the scope prefix 'it!x12345' to your respecting value)

entry.title: wage type is not picking up while running remittance
entry.content:
Hi Team

We have recently created new wage type. when I run PC00_M10_CALC, the amount shows up but when I run PC00_M99_URME the wage type does not show up, any configuration missing that should be aded or anything else to be done? please share your thoughts

Note- supplier is created for this wage type

Regards

Jithendra Shivashankar

entry.title: Re: Exception Subprocess for IDoc Receiver Adapter
entry.content:
Actually, just now solved it ourselves. The trick was to set the "Return HTTP Response Code as Header" checkbox in the receiver adapter. This forced the IDoc receiver adapter to go into the exception subprocess.

Thanks,

Sid

entry.title: Bex report key figures header text is changing when the language is changed
entry.content: When the language is changing to Spanish the FTQ report header key figure description is changing to selection1, selection2 form4 and form5. The expectation is that key figure description still needs to be shown in English language only. It is correctly working in production system. whereas it is not working in nonproduction system (BQ2). I have attached the issue details.

entry.title: Re: Exception Subprocess for IDoc Receiver Adapter
entry.content: Actually, solved it ourselves just now. The trick was to set the "Send HTTP Response as Header" checkbox in the adapter. This forced the IDoc adapter to go into the exception subprocess after an error.

entry.title: Re: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD EVENT - Billing Document Created
entry.content:
Hello @dawid,

Can you please let us know how we can add a new header text in billing document with the SSCUI 101292?

The sequence part is not that clear for us. If you heave any process to follow ? 

Thank you very much for your help.

BR

mouna

entry.title: Bex report key figures header text is changing when the language is changed
entry.content: During the execution of Bex report (FTQ report) in portal when the user changes the language to Spanish, key figure descriptions are converting to selection1, selection2 form4 and form5. The expectation of the business user is that key figure description still needs to be shown in English language only. It is correctly working in production system. whereas it is not working in nonproduction system (BQ2). I have attached the issue details.

entry.title: Challenges and Best Practices in HR Management Software Development Solutions
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m currently working at Dev Technosys, and our team has been actively developing workforce management platforms for various businesses. While working on HR Management software Development Solutions, we've encountered several challenges related to scalability, automation, and user adoption, and I'd love to hear insights from industry professionals and fellow developers.

We're aiming to build a platform comparable to what a modern HR Management software Development Company would deliver, but some implementation and operational questions have come up during the process.

Questions for the Community

  • What are the biggest challenges you've faced while building HR management systems?
  • How do you efficiently handle employee data security and compliance requirements?
  • What features have shown the highest adoption rates among HR teams?
  • Has anyone integrated AI capabilities into HR Management software Development Services for recruitment, employee engagement, or performance management?
  • What strategies work best for improving user experience across HR, managers, and employees?

Current Issues We're Facing

During HR Management software Development, our team has encountered a few common obstacles:

  • Managing large employee databases while maintaining system performance.
  • Creating seamless integrations with payroll, accounting, and attendance systems.
  • Handling role-based access controls and sensitive employee information.
  • Designing intuitive dashboards that serve multiple user types.
  • Ensuring compliance with changing labor laws and organizational policies.

Looking for Expert Recommendations

I would appreciate hearing from professionals who have worked on HR platforms or enterprise workforce management solutions.

  • Which technology stack has worked best for long-term scalability?
  • How do you approach cloud-based HR software architecture?
  • What reporting and analytics features are most valuable to HR teams?
  • Are there any implementation mistakes or lessons learned that others should be aware of?

Final Thoughts

At Dev Technosys, we are continuously exploring ways to improve workforce automation, employee engagement, and operational efficiency. However, balancing usability, security, and integration requirements remains a significant challenge.

I'd love to hear your experiences, recommendations, and best practices. Looking forward to learning from the community and discussing innovative approaches to modern HR software development.

Thanks in advance for your valuable insights!

entry.title: Re: Post Capitalization(Forgotten Asset)
entry.content:
Dear Nilam,

The depreciation values resulting from post-capitalization are not included in the current year’s depreciation calculation.

Depreciation values calculated for previous year are posted with the "post-capitalization" transaction (see ordinary depreciation section for the posted transaction in AW01N).

Depreciation values posted via the "post-capitalization" transaction are initially listed under the "value adjustment" line; however, they will be displayed under "ordinary depreciation" in the following fiscal year.

entry.title: GOS Attachments Migration from SAP ECC to S/4HANA and Storage in SharePoint
entry.content:
We are migrating from SAP ECC to S/4HANA. Currently, GOS attachments for FB03 are stored in SAP tables such as SOOD, SOFFPHIO, and SOFFCONT1.

Our requirement is to store all new GOS attachments directly in SharePoint instead of these SAP tables.

In addition, we would like to migrate existing GOS attachments from SAP to SharePoint while maintaining the attachment links and accessibility from FB03.

Has anyone implemented a similar solution? What is the recommended approach, tools, or SAP-supported integration for storing GOS attachments in SharePoint and migrating historical attachments?

Any guidance or best practices would be appreciated.

entry.title: Best practice for scope item 5W2 (Group Valuation) when future requirements are uncertain
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding scope item 5W2 – Group Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

As far as I understand, 5W2 can only be activated as part of the initial scoping and cannot be added later once the implementation has progressed.

We are currently working with a customer who does not require Group Valuation today. However, they cannot completely rule out that this requirement may arise in the future as their group structure and reporting requirements evolve.

In such a situation, what is SAP's recommended approach?

  • Is it considered a best practice to activate 5W2 proactively during the initial scoping phase, even if there is currently no confirmed business requirement?
  • Are there any disadvantages or additional complexities associated with activating 5W2 "just in case"?
  • How does SAP generally advise customers to handle scope items that cannot be activated later but may become relevant in the future?

I would appreciate any guidance, SAP recommendations, or lessons learned from project experience.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC - Driving Lite Viewer Adoption: Why Your Tables Matter
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

thank you very much for the hints how to distinguish Lite Viewer vs. Full Viewer.

For our test case it seems the full viewer is used.
No idea why (new table experience was enabled), no scripts, just a plain story (2 Tab Stripes with tables on a BW-Live model). Only non-ordinary is usage of Universal-Display-Hierarchy in the query.

So now I got the same question as Jef:
Is there a way to see what is preventing the usage of the Lite Viewer? 

Thanks and best regards
Axel

Best,

Kevin

In SBC,

There is a requirement, how to post the GR document with five months ago date.

entry.title: Disable Change Manager field in CRM Web UI
entry.content:
We are creating a new change document type in our Solman 7.2 system. In this new change document type we only need one node approval, our question is if it is possible to make change manager field not visible in General Data section.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disable Change Manager field in CRM Web UI
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Please:

  • Activate Configuration Mode in CRM Web UI
  • Click on the wheel icon and then anywhere on the General Data section
  • Select the desired row (Change Manager) and click on the delete icon
  • Click on "Save and Close" and choose a suitable TR

Additionally, you can unassign Change Manger in /nSPRO -> Define Partner Determination Procedure

entry.title: How to connect to INFORMATICA through Rest API in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

We want to connect to INFORMATICA data in SAC. As far as I know we can connect to INFORMATICA data in SAC either through Open Connector or through REST API. Can someone please help on how to connect through Rest API?

If the Informatica developer provides an API  can I consume in SAC ? 

Thanks in advance.

Regards

Ahmed Baseer

entry.title: Is a completed Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) mandatory before starting an PC Project?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a question regarding the role of the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects.

According to SAP Activate, the DDA is performed during the Discover phase and serves as the basis for defining scope, country versions, integrations, and the overall solution approach.

However, in practice, projects sometimes start with an incomplete or partially validated DDA due to timeline constraints, ongoing business decisions, or evolving requirements.

My questions are:

  • Is a completed and formally finalized DDA considered a mandatory prerequisite before starting an implementation project?
  • Does SAP have an official recommendation regarding projects that start without a fully completed DDA?
  • What are the main risks or implications from SAP's perspective if the implementation begins while parts of the DDA are still open or under discussion?
  • Have other customers or partners successfully started projects with an incomplete DDA, and if so, how was scope governance handled?

I would appreciate any SAP guidance, best practices, or project experiences.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Warehouse Task Concept in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Ed...
entry.content: Thank you sooo much this is veeeery helpful

entry.title: Freight Condition ZHD0 not distributing to all lines on Invoice
entry.content:
We have a Freight Condition getting updated on delivery header directly . It is coming from interface.

When the invoice is created with reference to delivery, the entire freight value sits on the last time of the invoice. (Please note I am not referring to rounding value but entire freight value)

When the credit memo is created only for the last time, then seems like we are giving the entire freight value back to customer instead of the proportionate value, which is wrong and when credit memo is created for other lines, then there is no credit given to customer for freight. 

However, when we put  Freight condition type on the sales order, then we see that it distributes the freight across all lines. Same information is passed to delivery/invoice. 

And when the credit memo is created for a specific line item, then the proportionate freight is given to customer, which is right. 

I dont think so there is a config issue with condition type as it is same being used on order & delivery. 

So, can you please confirm if this is how SAP Standard works and if there is a way to distribute the freight on all the lines on the invoice if the freight is polulated on delivery directly.

entry.title: Re: S_ALR-/IMG-Transaktionen als Launchpad App Descriptor...
entry.content:
Hello Joe,

Thank you for the detailed response.

I believe I have found a solution that works for my scenario. I created a catalog in the Launchpad Designer (/UI2/FLPD_CUST) containing the S_ALR* transactions that were not working correctly. Afterwards, I migrated this business catalog into a technical catalog using the migration tool (/UI2/MGR_TC), including the corresponding Launchpad App Descriptor Items.

This approach makes me independent of the Launchpad Designer in case it is no longer available in future releases.

The solution works well: from the migrated technical catalog, I can create references via the Content Manager and successfully launch the transactions.

The only drawback is that once the entries have been converted into technical catalogs, I can no longer modify them directly, as doing so would reintroduce the original issue. However, I can live with that limitation for now, since I only need to define the LADI correctly once and can then use it going forward.


Best regards,
Julian

entry.title: SAP successfactor
entry.content: SAP SuccessFactors is a powerful platform, but every organization uses it a little differently. I’m interested in hearing what part of SuccessFactors creates the most questions or challenges in your experience.

entry.title: Re: Week 2 – Building AI Agents with CrewAI and Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Week 2 - Solution:

entry.title: Re: Adding custom field in "Receive Products" app
entry.content:
Dear @Pyae_Phyo_Thwe,

For Look Up Retail Products SAP provides a defined set of released extensibility options in Public Cloud. You cannot modify the delivered app in place or freely add fields/buttons but it can be extended as follows:

  • UI: customize the app's user interface via a SAPUI5 adaptation project in SAP Business Application Studio (SAP Fiori tools). This creates an application variant the original app stays unchanged
  • Logic/behavior: four released BAdIs

Adding a new persistent custom field is not part of this app's documented extensibility. Eclipse/ADT and BAS cannot modify the delivered app directly only the released scenarios above

More details below:

SAP Help – Extensibility: Look Up Retail Products

SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library – Look Up Retail Products

Work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects

SAP Help Extending an SAP Fiori Application for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP BTP, ABAP Environment

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Learning Opportunities for Industries – Join the Live Session and Shape What’s Next
entry.content: We will run the next Live Session from the series Learning Opportunities for Industries on Thursday, June 25th, 2026, at 4:00 pm CEST. Add your questions as comments on the post, and we will answer them during the session and/or follow up afterward.

entry.title: Recommendation for configuration role based on standard template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts, 

We are creating the business roles in an S/4HANA Public Cloud system. We were planning to create a role for access to configure in implementation activities as a copy of the SAP standard business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT to grant access to all the configuration points. 

The problem we are facing is that the business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT in our system has almost 300 catalogs, and we get this message when working with the business role:

And then checking the Best Practices for Business Roles | SAP Help Portal in fact SAP is recommending "Not more than 100 business catalogs should be assigned to a business role".

My questions:

  1. Is there any SAP recommendation on how to handle business roles based on the business role template SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT?
  2. Is there any SAP recommendation on how to set up the security to grant permissions to update the configuration in an S/4HANA Public Cloud system? 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP S/4HANA on-premises with ActiveMQ on-pr...
entry.content:
Almost.

If you'd be wanting to use APIs of S/4HANA on-premise then you can use EIC to locally connect to them and consume/proxy the data.

If you'd be wanting to use Events of S/4HANA on-premise then you need an Event Broker. Two event-enabled systems do not connect to each other, they connect to a shared broker. You could look at Advanced Event Mesh which can have brokers locally installed in your K8S. Depending on what you want to do with the events, there might be no need for EIC (event consumer) if you already have another AMQP consumer as intended target.

The JMS adapter inside EIC is for internal async usage only (internal event broker to facilitate handover between iflows) and can not be used to connect to external event brokers (this is a roadmap item) and certainly not to event producers directly (non-brokers).

The AMQP adapter inside EIC can be used to connect to external AMQP-capable event brokers, such as Advanced Event Mesh.

entry.title: Re: ABAP Proxy generation support with SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi @Hari_narayanan25 ,

With Basic Authentication, you may not be able to use clientId and clientSecret. 

Logon Procedure  : Basic Authentication

Basic Authentication settings : Enter the credentials of the Technical User created with Default Identity Provider and with role AuthGroup_IntegrationDeveloper assigned.(Note : Do not use clientId and client secret from service key instance for Basic authentication)

entry.title: Dynamic Parameter needs credentials
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question. I have a report and I'd like to bind a dynamic parameter as a filter. So far so good, but when the reports is printed I get the DataBase prompt to sign in with credentials. The user does not have those credentials, the ERP is passing this while printing, but apparently not for the dynamic parameter, just for the report. Is there anyway to fix this or workaround?

Glad for any answers.

Kind regards

Samuel

entry.title: Attachments uploaded from SSAM with Arabic description is updated as ######## in backend system
entry.content:
Hi Experts,
While using the SSAM 2505 application, we are facing with Arabic descriptions of attachments uploaded from SSAM app, the description is updated as  ######### in the S4 backend system. 
We have implemented the SAP note : 3499745 - Add filter switch for encoding of Attachment filename and description, but the stll the issue is not resolved. 
I have enabled the backend gateway traces and checked, I can see Arabic description in the coming to backend  but in table is updated as #########. 
Our SAP backend system Unicode.

Can anyone help me with this issue. 

@bill_froelich @Jitendra_Kansal78

Thanks
Madhukar G

entry.title: Re: SAP .NET Connector not working anymore
entry.content: Thanks so far for your answer. When we have BW memory issue, we do get normally TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED. I already tried to increase the BW memory parameter but was not able to solved the issue. In this new issue, I have the feeling it comes more from the Nco but don´t know where I can look. There is a SAP note to setup the trace but somehow I didn´t find the file parameter.

entry.title: Re: RFC Connectivity Testing - JCo 3.1, and NCo 3.1, with...
entry.content:
Hi Chris,

  1. Yes.
  2. Yes. However, the application function modules you need for your scenario are most probably not available. 
  3. It makes sense to consider .NET 10 directly, as .NET 8 support will phase out at the end of the year.

Best regards,
Markus

entry.title: CALM: How is the Upgrade Roadmap used in parallel with an Implementation Roadmap?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Upgrade Roadmap states:

"The roadmap can be used as a standalone mini project for a customer who is already live with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, without ongoing projects. It can also be used in parallel, or have tasks added to an ongoing new implementation or continuous improvement project."

My understanding is therefore that SAP explicitly supports using upgrade activities during an ongoing implementation project.

However, in SAP Cloud ALM, a project appears to be assigned to a single SAP Activate roadmap.

My question is:

How is SAP's recommendation implemented in SAP Cloud ALM when upgrade activities need to be managed in parallel with an ongoing implementation?

For example:

  • Do you create a second SAP Cloud ALM project based on the Upgrade Roadmap?
  • Do you manually add the upgrade tasks to the implementation project?
  • Is there any way to consume both roadmaps within the same project?

I would appreciate any guidance from SAP or implementation partners who have managed Public Cloud projects across mandatory SAP release upgrades.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule for Developers, ABAP AI capabilities including agentic capabilities is now available!
entry.content: hi @Swathy_S , Customer is having below application,  activation of below application  will enable the customer to use the J4D capability in S/4 HANA 2025 FPS 01  without doing any configuration in S/4 Side as earlier we need activate the J4D service in SAP BTP  account as well as preform the steps in S/4 side where customer is entitled.

entry.title: Re: Subject: Unable to Access SAP Cloud Identity Services...
entry.content: I am also facing this issue.

entry.title: Issue with Dynpro Screen Download/Upload Between Systems After Namespace Change
entry.content:
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.


Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?
We migrated a custom SAP development from System A to System B and changed the namespace during the process.

Example:
/ABC/PROGRAM → YXYZ_PROGRAM

Most objects work fine after renaming. However, when we download a Dynpro/screen from System A and upload it into System B, we get a “screen overflow” error. Even after updating all obvious references to the new namespace, the screen still doesn’t work correctly.

Has anyone encountered screen overflow or Dynpro upload issues after a namespace change? Are there hidden dependencies in screens that don’t get updated automatically during download/upload?

entry.title: Trial account -Unable to login into SAP BTP Cloud Identity services admin cockpit after subscribing
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am Unable to login into SAP BTP Cloud Identity services admin cockpit after subscribing to it in my trial account.

Please let me know, for the possible solution.

entry.title: New Company - Document Numbering
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I am setting up a new company with a blank database for the 1st time.

On the numbering series - setup screen, I dont see all of the marketing document.  When I put in transactions, its using the same sequence for all documents, so PO 1000, GRPO 1001, PO1002, SO1003

Is this the setting I need to uncheck to show the categories?  If so, then do I need to start with a blank database?


Thanks in advance for your help...


Brian Branco
